Practice Schedule

Find your practice times, locations, and learn what to expect at Lansing Area Track Club training sessions.

Weekly Schedule

Practice schedules by age group. All schedules are subject to change based on weather and facility availability.

Youth (Ages 6-12)

Tuesday & Thursday

5:00 PM - 6:30 PM

Sexton High School Track

Focus: Fundamentals, fun activities, basic technique

Middle School (Ages 12-14)

Monday, Wednesday & Friday

4:30 PM - 6:00 PM

Eastern High School Track

Focus: Progressive training, event introduction, conditioning

High School Prep (Ages 14-18)

Monday - Friday

4:00 PM - 6:00 PM

Everett High School Track

Focus: Advanced training, event specialization, competition prep

What to Bring

Make sure your athlete is prepared for every practice session.

  • Comfortable athletic clothing
  • Running shoes (spikes optional for advanced athletes)
  • Water bottle
  • Sunscreen (for outdoor practices)
  • Positive attitude!

What to Expect

Here is what a typical practice looks like at LATC.

1

Arrive On Time

Please arrive 10-15 minutes early to check in and warm up with the group.

2

Be Prepared

Bring water, appropriate shoes, and dress for the weather conditions.

3

Stay Positive

Encourage teammates and maintain a growth mindset during training.

4

Listen to Coaches

Follow instructions and ask questions when you need clarification.

Typical Practice Structure

Every practice follows a structured format to maximize development and safety.

1. 0-15 min

Check-in & Dynamic Warm-up

Athletes check in with coaches and begin group warm-up exercises.

2. 15-30 min

Drills & Technique Work

Focused drills to improve running form, starts, and event-specific skills.

3. 30-60 min

Main Workout

Event-specific training, conditioning, or speed work based on the day's plan.

4. 60-75 min

Cool Down & Stretch

Light jogging and stretching to aid recovery.

5. 75-90 min

Team Talk & Dismissal

Coaches share announcements and athletes are dismissed to parents.

Ready to Get Started?

Register your athlete today and join us at our next practice.

Register Now

Lansing Area

Track Club

Helping youth athletes in Lansing, Michigan build speed, endurance, confidence, and discipline through track, running, and community since 2015.

Serving Lansing, East Lansing, Okemos, Holt, Waverly, Delta Township and surrounding Mid-Michigan communities.
